notes: Roman provincial governor praised by Isidore of Pelusium and mentioned by Jerome around 410 AD.

docs.title: Letter CXVIII
docs.author: Jerome
docs.excerpt: … here is our Ausonius beginning to be impatient for the sheets, hurrying the secretaries, and in his impatience at the neighing of his horse, accusing my poor wits of slowness. Remember me, then, and prosper in Christ.

docs.title: Letter 125
docs.author: Isidore of Pelusium
docs.excerpt: The person who not only defends those who have been wronged but also takes care that no one is wronged in the first place — he deserves to be called not only an effective ruler but also a wise and just lawgiver.

docs.title: Letter 1016
docs.author: Quintus Aurelius Symmachus
docs.subject_summary: gnstias] ♦**♦♦ {U) tibi] ***** (II) 5 res secunda] ***** (J7) 7 reside V, om. M
docs.excerpt: Did I seem to you too unrefined to appreciate it, or too stingy to praise it? Either way, you've seriously underestimated my talents or my character. And yet, despite your embargo, I managed — barely — to get access to the poem.
